description: Add email verification to users table — review a migration adding columns and dropping a column on a high-traffic users table with 12M rows under zero-downtime rolling deployment
review_checklist: Check for NOT NULL columns added without DEFAULT values on large tables
Verify indexes are created with CONCURRENTLY on large tables
Check if dropped or renamed columns are still referenced by application code
Check for lock-heavy operations on high-traffic tables
Consider the deployment strategy (rolling/blue-green) and downtime budget

ground_truth.description: Adding NOT NULL column email_verified without a DEFAULT on a 12M-row table causes a full table rewrite with ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock, blocking all reads and writes. Use DEFAULT FALSE for a metadata-only operation in PostgreSQL 11+.
Creating index without CONCURRENTLY on a 12M-row table acquires a SHARE lock, blocking all writes for the duration of the index build.

Dropping legacy_auth_token while auth_service still queries it in validate_legacy_token() and migrate_to_new_auth(). In a rolling deployment, old code will fail immediately after the column is removed.
